measured.transcript: Today is Wednesday, May the third, two thousand six. The time is approximately eight thirty three PM The location is twenty eight eleven West Main Street, Casper, Wyoming. My name is Greg Irwin, video specialist of Legal Video of Wyoming, located at three fifty Katie Lane, Casper, Wyoming. This is case number five nine eight dash six three six nine filed in the district court in Toronto County, Wyoming, Eighth Judicial District, The deponent is Paul Fraser. The video deposition is requested by John Spencer, Attorney for the Defense. court reporter is Dorothy Bunn of Dor of Bunn and Counsel and all present will please identify themselves for the record. John Spencer representing the Max Mutual Insurance Company. Do you mind if your witness does not stand to be sworn? No, I do not. do you solemnly swear from the testimony I do. Thank you. We're on the record. Mr. Fraser, my name is John Spencer. I represent the Max Mutual Insurance Company. I'll be asking a series of questions in regard to your knowledge as to the operations of the TRZ plant, which will have a bearing on the outcome of a lawsuit pending between the TRZ corporation and the Max Mutual Insurance Company. I will ask you to wait to answer until the entire question has been asked, then you must answer verbally, as the court reporter cannot record your nonverbal answers. I will also wait until you have Mr. Fraser, is there any reason that you cannot fulfill your obligations No. you taking any medicine that could affect your ability to answer my questions today. No. Thank you. Now let me advise you that you are under oath and you're to answer every question to the best of your ability And as accurately as possible. If you do not understand the question, please ask me to explain it further and I'll be happy to do so. If you cannot answer a question accurately, then we are entitled to your best estimate. that clear? Yes. Good. Now will you please state your full name and address for the record. Paul Robert Frazier, South Third Street, Douglas, Wyoming And how long have you lived there? About fifteen years. We moved there when I went to work for the TRZ corporation. How old are you, Mr. Fraser? Fifty three. Are you married, Mr Fraser? Yes to to the same woman for twenty seven years. Do you have any children? Objection, irrelevant. You may answer, Mr. Frazier. Yes, we have three, two boys and a girl, but uh they're all married and live elsewhere. Mr. Fraser, what is your educational background I graduated from high school in Boston and went to RPI. I got a BS in geophysics in 1975. Since then I took several postgraduate courses and received my master's from the University of five years later. And when did you join the TRZ I believe it was in the fall of nineteen ninety. What was your position at that time? I was a project engineer. was your position when you left TRZ? I was chief engineer and head of the engineering department. What year was that? Two thousand and four. How long had you been in that position? For about the last five years of my employment. In that position, Mr. Fraser, Were you responsible for the proper treatment and disposal of the waste material that came from the manufacturing process? I I drew up the specifications for the treatment, but I was not responsible for the procedures. Who would have been responsible for the final disposition of the waste? That would have been the head of the waste management department. who would have been responsible to see to it that the head of that department was properly following your waste treatment procedure. assume that would have been the plant's general manager. Do you know who that might have been? No, there were several different employees that held that position over the years. Yes, I do believe he held that position I'd like to request that we go off the record at this time so that I may uh take this phone call. approximately 838 PM. We are back on the record the time's approximately 841 p.m. Okay Mr. Fraser, do you consider yourself as an expert in your field? Objection. Mr. Frazier's not here as an expert witness. He's a previous employee who's been called on to testify as to his knowledge of the subject of this lawsuit and nothing more. Mr. Frazier, please answer the question. Well, I don't work in that anymore, so no, I would not consider to be an expert. Well Have considered yourself an expert at the time of your employment, isn't that right, Mr. Fraser? Objection leading. Mr. Fraser, let me get this clear. Are you saying that Ms. Wagner has covered the questions that you're going to be asked and instructed you on how she expects you to answer them? um she told me that there was a uh a Million dollar cleanup pending against the the plant, and i i it it was depending upon uh how I answered the questions at the at this deposition. of what Ms. Wagner has told you about this case, do you feel that you have to protect the company by the way you answer my questions? Spencer, I object to that whole line of questioning. I'd like to go off the record and have a conference I think so, Ms. Wagner. It looks to me like there's already been too much conversation between you and the witness. what has Ms. Wagner told you regarding this case? Mr. I don't want you to answer that. I'm bringing this deposition to a close right now, and I'm again asking that we go off the record so that I can call the judge and ask him to rule very clear Ms. Wagner, you will be responsible for all future costs in regard to the taking of testimony of this witness. And may I forewarn you that if you continue to coach the witness, it will be treated very seriously as a possible obstruction justice. And if so, we intend to pursue it to the fullest extent of the law. Do you understand that? Yes, Mr. Spencer, and I don't need you to teach me the law. We'll let the judge determine just who's right and wrong in this line of questioning. Under those circumstances, I agree that we will adjourn the deposition and go off the record at this time. We will reconvene after the judge has an opportunity to Of my line of questioning. May be assured, Ms. Wagner, that we intend to continue questioning Mr. Fraser until he has an opportunity to answer all our questions in this matter. We're now going off the record. concludes tape one, volume one of the videotape deposition of Mr. Paul Frazier. The original of today's testimony will remain in the custody of Legal Video of Wyoming, address is 350 Katie Lane, Casper, Wyoming, 82601. The time is approximately 844 PM. We are off the record
